ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
Off Calendar Jan-31-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lyndon-Lam, via ZOOM, for Abbas Fard, Administrator.

The court asks counsel regarding the bank statement that shows Abbas Fard as Conservator. Counsel reports that his client does not recall deposits showing himself as Conservator and advises that this may have been caused by a mislabeling at the bank. The court asks counsel if he wishes to further supplement the accounting. Counsel advises that he does not.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lyndom-Lam, via ZOOM, for Abbas Fard, Personal Representative.

The court begins by noting that there are filings pending in the court's e-filing queue. Counsel confirms that he has submitted an amended petition and verified bank statements for e-filing and advises that notices will need to be re-issued once the amended petition is filed.

The Accounting is continued to September 15, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Filing of the above-referenced documents will be set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Apr-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bobby Lydon-Lam appeared by zoom for Abbas Fard (Administrator).

The Court noted that the hold up in closing this estate is the selling of the liquor license and that the liquor license is not listed as an asset on the inventory and appraisal. Mr. Lydon-Lam stated that at first they weren't sure if they were going to be able to sell the liquor license since it is tied to a physical address that wasn't the Decedent's, and that the Family has spoken with a Broker of these types of licenses and they are able to sell.
The Court informed Mr. Lydon-Lam that it is going to set a status hearing for the final inventory which is to include the liquor license valued at the Decedent's date of death and noted that this estate is now at a two-year mark. Mr. Lydon-Lam stated that if they are able to sell the liquor license before the two-year mark, he will do a final petition. The Court informed Mr. Lydon-Lam that the estate is already at the two-year mark and that he will need to submit a two-year accounting. Status on filing of the final inventory and appraisal is set for June 2, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the first accounting is also set for June 2, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the documents are filed by May 26, 2021, no appearance will be required. The Amended Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.
